author: codex-auto-release

patch.value: Deliver a scheduled and manually testable release pipeline that skips when no commits exist after the latest release tag, computes the YYYY.M.D[-N] version, regenerates the full changelog from pm items, runs release gates, commits and tags safely, publishes to the npm registry, and proves both Node and Bun consumer execution. Preserve every fact currently present in CHANGELOG.md by backfilling missing pm items before generator ownership begins.
Automate release publication after repository changes

Mirror pm-cli's daily change-aware release automation for monica-cli, generate CHANGELOG.md from complete pm lineage, publish once to npm with provenance, and verify the exact package through npm/npx and Bun/bunx.

No-change runs exit successfully without creating a tag; Every existing CHANGELOG.md fact is represented by closed pm lineage; Generated CHANGELOG.md is reproducible with pm-changelog; Tag publication verifies npm metadata, npx execution, and bunx execution
Eliminate manual release drift while retaining complete historical release evidence.
Every eligible master change can become a verified npm/Bun-consumable release without silent changelog loss.
Daily scheduled runs either publish one verified release or report a deterministic no-change skip.
The repository has unreleased July changes and a manual-only release workflow.

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: Reference implementation is pm-cli auto-release plus tag-triggered release workflow.

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: CHANGELOG.md preservation is a hard migration invariant; generator output is accepted only after semantic comparison.

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: Bun consumes npm-registry packages; dual support is one provenance publication plus npx and bunx verification.

message: Track complete auto-release and changelog-governance delivery

author: codex-auto-release
message: Replace version-mutating production gate with runnable current-head checks

value.command: bun run changelog:pm:check && bun run changelog:preservation:check
bun run typecheck && bun run lint && bun run build && bun run test

author: codex-auto-release

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: Verified the existing public 2026.3.6-2 package end to end: npm metadata and integrity resolved, npx and bunx both returned 2026.3.6-2 on their first attempt, and the GitHub release is final. The release environment currently contains NPM_TOKEN but not RELEASE_PAT; production Auto Release intentionally fails fast until a maintainer adds the protected-master bypass token.

message: Record public surface proof and required secret state

author: codex-auto-release

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: GitHub Actions design was grounded in the official workflow syntax and Node package publishing documentation. The publish job uses contents:write, id-token:write, setup-node registry configuration, NODE_AUTH_TOKEN, and npm publish --provenance. Bun documentation confirms bunx installs from npm and the default Bun registry is registry.npmjs.org, so dual runtime support is verified consumption rather than duplicate publication.

message: Document authoritative workflow and registry rationale

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: The newest calendar release tag v2026.3.6-2 is not an ancestor of current master after historical integration. Release detection must select the maximum calendar-version tag from all tags rather than relying on git describe, then diff that tag tree against HEAD.

message: Record external activation blocker after local and public-surface verification

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: Credential activation blocker cleared: the npm account was validated and NPM_TOKEN plus RELEASE_PAT were configured in the release environment. Secret values remain outside tracked files and pm metadata.

patch.value: The protected release environment now contains both required secret names, and npm authentication was validated without persisting secret values.

author: codex-auto-release

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: Public pm data was scanned against the configured credential values and common secret formats; no credential material was found. Runtime checkpoints are ignored and are not part of the committed pm data.

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: Final linked verification passed typecheck, lint, build, all 547 tests, workflow validation, and targeted release tests. The changelog check correctly detected the newly closed credential item and will be regenerated after this feature close so both outcomes are included.

message: Record final verification and public-data safety evidence

patch.value: Implemented the pm-cli-equivalent scheduled release orchestrator, tag publication workflow, full-history pm-changelog migration, preservation guard, exact registry/runtime verification, and release runbook.
Changed master heads produce one verified calendar-version release; unchanged or tracker-only heads skip; all prior changelog facts remain represented by closed pm items.

Local and isolated proofs validate changed, unchanged, and tracker-only paths; 547 tests and release gates pass; public package verification succeeds through npm, npx, bunx, and GitHub; required environment secrets are configured.
Change-aware npm release automation, pm-generated lossless changelog ownership, Bun consumer verification, credentials, tests, and operator documentation are complete.

author: codex-auto-release

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: Release metadata derived from workflow or CLI input should still be validated and compared literally when possible; constructing a regular expression from version input adds unnecessary injection risk.

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: GitHub Advanced Security identified a high-severity regular-expression injection path in release-note heading lookup. Replaced the dynamic expression with validated calendar-version input and exact heading comparisons; valid extraction and malicious-pattern rejection both pass locally.

message: Record and resolve CodeQL release-note finding

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: A clean-tree precondition makes git add -u the safer release-commit contract: it captures all intentional tracked mutations while excluding unexpected untracked files.

value.author: codex-auto-release
value.text: Gemini review identified brittle hardcoded release staging. The pipeline now stages every modified tracked file with git add -u after its clean-tree precondition, so future version synchronization outputs cannot be silently omitted and untracked artifacts remain excluded.
